scope: Broader positive tid sweep summary across 420-445 and 530-550, plus later 641-647 jump-scan and 636-640/648-652 flanking follow-up around the current 642 local anomaly
verdict: API1 still shows only one true positive content branch on `tid=431` within the broader 420-445 and 530-550 sweep, while `tid=537` remains a separate `success_shell_without_sample` branch.
API2 now has confirmed positive branches on `tid=535`, `tid=540`, and newly `tid=541` in both XML and JSONP wrappers.
The new `tid=541` API2 branch is not a one-sample accident: it also stays positive on a second sample (`vid=j4101ouc4ve`), while API1 `tid=541` stays on the `key all illegal` branch.

Within the current broadened upper window, `tid=542-550` fall back to standard `-111005` on API2 XML/JSONP, so the 54x positive edge is currently narrowed to `540-541` rather than an open-ended upward run.

The later 522-525 cross-interface follow-up does not add a new positive hit either: 522-524 stay on ordinary `-111005`, while 525 is now confirmed as a cross-interface `-111013` island on API1, API2 XML, and API2 JSONP across two public samples.

The later `636-640` and `648-652` flanking follow-up returns standard `-111005` on API1 and API2 XML/JSONP throughout, which strengthens the reading that `642` is currently a local anomaly island rather than the edge of a broader positive cluster or a wider `-111013` family.
